title: Event Recommendation System
description: This project was my final thesis for the completion of my bachelor's degree at Universitas Multimedia Nusantara. Event-Hunters is a mock event search web application that uses a hybrid recommendation system to help users discover relevant events more effectively.

sections.label: Introduction
sections.heading: Why this recommendation project was built
sections.summary: A quick overview of the thesis background, the user problem, and the goal of Event-Hunters.
body: Event Recommendation System was developed as my final thesis project during my bachelor's degree at Universitas Multimedia Nusantara.
The main idea behind Event-Hunters was to build a mock event discovery platform that could reduce the effort users spend when searching through large lists of events manually.
Instead of showing the same content to everyone, the project explored how a hybrid recommendation approach could present events that feel more relevant to each individual user.

sections.label: Main Content
sections.heading: How Event-Hunters approached recommendations
sections.summary: The core section explains the platform flow, the hybrid recommendation idea, and the product screens that supported the user journey.
body: The application was designed as a mock event search experience where users could browse, search, and receive recommended events based on a hybrid recommendation strategy.
This hybrid approach was chosen to balance multiple recommendation signals instead of relying on a single method, allowing the system to produce more useful suggestions for different user contexts.
From the homepage to the search and recommendation results pages, each screen was built to support a clear flow: discover events, evaluate options, and move toward more personalized recommendations.

sections.label: Conclusion
sections.heading: What this project represents in my portfolio
sections.summary: A short reflection on the final outcome, what I learned, and why this project remains meaningful.
body: This project became an important milestone in my portfolio because it combined academic research, product thinking, and practical interface design in one end-to-end case study.
Working on Event-Hunters helped me think more carefully about how recommendation logic connects with the actual experience users see on screen.
More than a thesis deliverable, the project reflects my interest in building digital products that are both technically thoughtful and genuinely helpful for users.
